user_message: Working on Evergreen Solutions's territory planning. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 14:30 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 16:54 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 15:59 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-G trap (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-G failure mode (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-G -- Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence
